title: Aranet Probe Battery Percent and Stale Telemetry Gap Detection
app: Aranet Cloud → HEC, or MQTT ingest (Splunkbase #1890 / #7575)
spl:
index=environment sourcetype=aranet:sensor sensor_id=*
| stats latest(battery_pct) as batt, latest(_time) as last_seen by sensor_id, location
| eval hours_since_seen=round((now()-last_seen)/3600,2)
| where batt < 15 OR hours_since_seen > 2
| table sensor_id, location, batt, hours_since_seen
| sort hours_since_seen desc
description: Aranet probes run on primary cells; when battery percentage falls or uplinks stop while gateways remain healthy, environmental compliance dashboards go stale. Combining `battery_pct` with time-since-last-event differentiates dying sensors from broker outages.
value: Prevents undetected excursions in GxP or food storage by forcing battery swaps before telemetry gaps exceed SOP limits.
implementation: Extract `battery_pct` from Aranet JSON (`battery` or `batteryVoltage` mapped to percent if needed). Ingest at least hourly. Alert when `battery_pct < 15` or no event for >2 hours while peer sensors on the same `gateway_id` continue reporting—indicates dead node versus gateway fault.
visualization: Table (sensors stale or low battery), Timeline (gaps), Single value (count at risk).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Inventory lookup with expected reporting cadence per sensor class.
• Exclude sensors intentionally shelved.

Step 1 — Use `bucket` command for gap detection across irregular MQTT bursts if needed.

Step 2 — Pair with gateway health UC in network category.

Step 3 — Validate by removing battery in lab.

Step 4 — CMMS integration for work orders.
grandmaExplanation: We make sure each little sensor is still checking in and isn’t about to die from a low battery, so temperature records stay trustworthy.
